description: Calibration test cases for the /consolidate-memory script. Each case spins up a sandbox memory directory under os.tmpdir(), seeds it from the `fixtures` block, runs the script with the named subcommand+args, then asserts on exit code, stdout/stderr substrings, and on-disk file state. Fixtures are drawn from real Rare.lab terminology so keyword overlap and citation-grep tests behave realistically.

frontmatter.name: Captain Red
frontmatter.description: Founder and product visionary. Designer who verifies output visually. Address as Red.

memoryFiles.body: Captain Red is the founder and product visionary. Designer, not a developer. Verifies output visually. Always address as Red or Captain Red.

frontmatter.name: Ten-Ten Format Rule
frontmatter.description: Mandatory response format max 10 sentences max 10 words per sentence overrides every other length rule

memoryFiles.body: Every response must follow ten-ten format. Maximum 10 bullets or sentences. Each sentence maximum 10 words. No walls of text. Bullets only.

frontmatter.name: Two Line Answers
frontmatter.description: Crisp answers max 1 to 3 bullets or 2 lines of prose total

memoryFiles.body: Crisp 1 to 3 bullets max. 2 lines of prose maximum. No padding. Lead with the answer.

frontmatter.name: Concise Responses
frontmatter.description: Bulleted responses only no walls of text concise format

memoryFiles.body: Bulleted responses only. No walls of text. Concise above all. Structure scannable.

frontmatter.name: Shader Pre-Mortem
frontmatter.description: Five question pre-mortem before any shader edit covers fragColor downstream blend alpha

memoryFiles.body: Before editing any shader answer five questions. What does fragColor output. Who reads it downstream. Will mask alpha propagate. Will blend modes composite. Does it compile in GLSL ES 3.00.

frontmatter.name: No Background Agents
frontmatter.description: Never use background or parallel agents Captain Red wants Claude to do all work personally

memoryFiles.body: Never use background agents or parallel subagents. Do everything personally line by line.

frontmatter.name: Compiler V3 Pipeline Stages
frontmatter.description: V3 compiler eight stages normalize validate cull analyze liveness FBO schedule engineGen wrappers

memoryFiles.body: V3 compiler runs eight stages. Normalize. Validate. Cull. Analyze DAG. Liveness. FBO allocate. Schedule. EngineGen plus wrappers.

frontmatter.name: Compiler Strategy Settled
frontmatter.description: V3 production V4 quarantined for Year 3 decision made after peer review

memoryFiles.body: V3 is production. V4 quarantined as Year 3 architecture. All export and publish features use compiler-v3.

frontmatter.name: Daily Build Log
frontmatter.description: Day by day log of build activity

memoryFiles.body: April 9 entry. Built one thing. Fixed another. End of log.

frontmatter.name: Socket Map Authoritative
frontmatter.description: lib/graph/socketMap.ts is the single source of truth for socket to uniform mapping

memoryFiles.body: socketMap.ts authoritative. Never inline socket strings outside it.

frontmatter.name: Distinct Topic A
frontmatter.description: Highly specific rule about a unique topic with no overlap to communication or shaders

memoryFiles.body: Distinct topic A body. Nothing overlaps with anything else.

frontmatter.name: Distinct Topic B
frontmatter.description: Different specific rule about a different unique topic

memoryFiles.body: Distinct topic B body. Nothing overlaps with anything else.

frontmatter.name: Distinct Topic C
frontmatter.description: Yet another isolated rule with no shared keywords

memoryFiles.body: Distinct topic C body. Independent from the others.

fixtures.memoryIndex:
# Memory Index

## User

## Feedback - Communication

- [feedback_ten_ten_format.md](feedback_ten_ten_format.md) - ten-ten format ceiling
- [feedback_two_line_answers.md](feedback_two_line_answers.md) - two line answers
- [feedback_concise_responses.md](feedback_concise_responses.md) - concise responses

## Feedback - Engineering

- [feedback_shader_premortem.md](feedback_shader_premortem.md) - 5 question shader pre-mortem

## Feedback - Agent Behavior

- [feedback_no_background_agents.md](feedback_no_background_agents.md) - never run parallel agents

## Reference

## Project

## Misc

- [feedback_distinct_topic_b.md](feedback_distinct_topic_b.md) - distinct topic B

citationFiles.content:
# Project rules

See node_graph_socket_map.md for the canonical socket map.
The shader pre-mortem in feedback_shader_premortem.md is mandatory.

# GLSL rules

R06: see feedback_shader_premortem.md for the 5 question gate.

# Agent rules

R17 plan-before-code. No parallel agents - cf feedback_no_background_agents.md.

cases.rationale: Dry-run must never write to disk. Every fixture file should be byte-identical after the run.
Migration maps the type field to the tier field per the canonical taxonomy: feedback=episodic, project=semantic, reference=procedural, user=core.
Migration only touches frontmatter. The markdown body below the closing --- must be byte-identical before and after.

args: A brand new rule for testing
Body text for the new rule. Short and clear.
cases.rationale: Store creates the file with correct frontmatter (including derived tier) and atomically appends a MEMORY.md pointer.
Storing under a name that already exists must halt with a clear error and leave the existing file plus index untouched.
Query terms intersect heavily with the shader pre-mortem keywords. It must rank top-1.
The type=reference filter must exclude project-typed files even when keyword overlap is high.
Read-only retrieval must not bump access_count or last_accessed. This is the audit/inspection mode.
Update touches only the YAML frontmatter. Body bytes below the closing --- must be identical.
Protected entries cannot be modified without explicit --force. The update must abort and the file stay untouched.
The three communication-format files share keywords (concise, bullets, format, length) above the 60% threshold. Summarize in detect-only mode must surface them as a cluster.
The three distinct-topic files share no keywords. They must not be flagged as a cluster.
Daily build log is uncited, low importance. Discard must move it to the archive directory and remove its MEMORY.md entry.
node_graph_socket_map.md is cited from CLAUDE.md. Citation grep must catch the reference and block the discard.
Predicate discard without --confirm must list candidates only and execute nothing. No file may be moved or archived.
Even with a permissive predicate, protected: true entries must be filtered out of any candidate set.
If the archive write fails (sim: read-only archive dir), the source file and MEMORY.md must both be untouched. No partial state.
Citation scan must walk the configured citation roots and report the rule file that mentions feedback_no_background_agents.
